title: JData JSON Schema
description: JSON schema for JData - a general-purpose data annotation and interchange format (Draft-4)

DataGroupDef.title: DataGroup
DataGroupDef.description: Container for organizing logically connected datasets

DatasetDef.title: Dataset
DatasetDef.description: Container for logically connected data records

Version.description: Version string of the data or the generating software

GeneratedBy.description: Name and version of the software that produced the data

DerivedFrom.description: URI or identifier of the source data from which this was derived

DataSchemaDef.title: DataSchema
DataSchemaDef.description: Associates a JSON Schema document with a data node for formal structure validation. The value must be either a valid JSON Schema object or a URI string pointing to an external schema document. Parsers that do not recognize _DataSchema_ must silently ignore it.

oneOf.description: URI pointing to an external JSON Schema document
Inline JSON Schema object

DataLinkDef.title: DataLink
DataLinkDef.description: Reference to internal or external data

oneOf.description: URI path to referenced data
Local index vector reference

Parameters.description: JSONPath expression to a specific element

MaxRecursion.description: Maximum recursion depth for link resolution

ArrayTypeDef.description: Numeric data type. float16/float32/float64 are aliases for half/single/double (NumPy/Arrow interoperability). byte/char/logical are aliases for uint8.

_ArrayOrder_.description: Array serialization order: row-major (r/row, default) or column-major (c/col/column)

_ArrayData_.description: Raw array data. When _ArrayChunks_ is present, this is a 1-D array of per-chunk payloads in row-major order.

_ArrayIsComplex_.description: True if array contains complex numbers. _ArrayData_ is a 2-D array whose first row holds the serialized real part and second row holds the serialized imaginary part of the complex array (separate rows, not byte-interleaved).

_ArrayIsSparse_.description: True if array is sparse (row-indices, col-indices, values format)

_ArrayLabel_.description: Dimension labels. Each element is a string name or a [name, start, width] tuple.

_ArrayCoords_.description: Positional coordinate array, one element per dimension in _ArrayLabel_ order (requires _ArrayLabel_). Each element's length must equal the corresponding _ArraySize_ entry. Elements may be plain 1-D arrays or annotated JData array objects; when _ArrayShape_:"range" is used, _ArrayData_ must be a 2-element [start, end] vector and _ArraySize_ must equal the dimension size.

oneOf.description: Plain 1-D coordinate array
Annotated JData array (preserves binary type information)
Single unit string applied uniformly to all dimensions (UDUNITS-2 convention, e.g. "mm", "ms", "kg/m^3"). An empty string denotes dimensionless or undefined.
Per-dimension unit strings with length equal to length(_ArraySize_)

_ArrayUnits_.description: Physical unit(s) of the array dimensions (UDUNITS-2 convention)

_ArrayFillValue_.description: Scalar sentinel for missing or invalid entries of the same numeric type as _ArrayType_. Important for integer types where IEEE 754 NaN cannot be represented. Both _ArrayFillValue_ and NaN entries (if present) are treated as missing.

oneOf.description: Shape ID followed by optional numeric parameters (e.g. bandwidth for band/toeplitz)

_ArrayShape_.description: Special matrix or array shape descriptor. Shapes: diag, upper/lower (triangular), uppersymm/lowersymm, band variants, toeplitz, circulant, hankel, identity, zero, range.

_ArrayChunks_.description: Tile (chunk) shape for partitioning an array into independently compressible blocks. Two modes depending on array type: (1) Dense real/complex arrays: _ArrayChunks_ is in _ArraySize_ (original) order; the decoder computes tile counts as ceil(_ArraySize_ / _ArrayChunks_) and tiles the row-major permuted intermediate. (2) Non-dense arrays (sparse, complex-sparse, shaped, complex-shaped): _ArrayChunks_ is in _ArrayZipSize_ (processed) coordinate space; the decoder computes tile counts as ceil(_ArrayZipSize_ / _ArrayChunks_). When present, _ArrayZipData_ becomes a 1-D array of per-chunk payloads in row-major tile order. The last chunk along any dimension may be smaller than the declared shape.

_ArrayZipType_.description: Compression codec identifier. Note: zlib (RFC 1950) and gzip (RFC 1952) are distinct formats. Only Blosc2 (not Blosc v1) is supported. 'blosc2' defaults to the BloscLZ internal codec; 'blosc2lz4' selects LZ4, 'blosc2lz4hc' LZ4-HC, 'blosc2zstd' Zstandard, 'blosc2zlib' zlib. Additional codec-specific parameters (typesize, clevel, shuffle, nthreads) may be passed via _ArrayZipOptions_.

oneOf.description: Shape of the pre-processed array (multi-dimensional form)
Total element count of the pre-processed array (scalar shorthand for 1-D case)

_ArrayZipSize_.description: Shape of the full pre-processed array before compression. For dense real arrays: [1, N] where N = total elements. For dense complex arrays: [2, N] where N = total elements. For non-dense arrays (sparse, shaped): the logical 2-D processed-array shape (e.g. [3, K] for a real sparse matrix with K nonzeros). When _ArrayChunks_ is absent, the decoder uses this shape to reconstruct the flat byte stream. When _ArrayChunks_ is present for dense arrays, tile counts are ceil(_ArraySize_ / _ArrayChunks_); for non-dense arrays, tile counts are ceil(_ArrayZipSize_ / _ArrayChunks_).

oneOf.description: Base64-encoded compressed byte stream (unchunked array)
Base64-encoded compressed payload for one chunk
_DataLink_ reference to an externally stored chunk payload (enables distributed/lazy-loaded chunk storage)
1-D array of per-chunk compressed payloads in row-major order (when _ArrayChunks_ is present). Each element is either a Base64-encoded string or a _DataLink_ pointing to an external chunk.

_ArrayZipData_.description: Compressed and Base64-encoded array data, or a 1-D cell of per-chunk payloads when _ArrayChunks_ is present

_ArrayZipEndian_.description: Byte order of the array elements before compression (default: little-endian)

_ArrayZipOptions_.description: Additional codec-specific parameters (e.g., for Blosc2: typesize, clevel, shuffle, nthreads)

_ArrayShuffle_.description: Byte or bit shuffle applied before compression. A positive integer N specifies byte shuffle with stride N (e.g., 4 reorders [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,...] to [1,5,...,2,6,...,3,7,...,4,8,...]). A negative integer -N specifies bit-wise shuffle with spacing N. Must be applied before compression; unshuffle applied on decompression.

oneOf.description: Column/row name (plain string form)

DataType.description: Data type of this column/row. datetime values are ISO 8601 strings; values without an explicit time-zone offset are interpreted as UTC.

_TableRecords_.description: 2D array storing table entries (rows of column values)

oneOf.description: Single column name forming the unique row index
Ordered list of column names forming a composite unique row index

_TableIndex_.description: Names of one or more columns in _TableCols_ that together form the unique row index (analogous to a SQL primary key or pandas DataFrame.index). If absent, the implicit index is the 1-based row position.

_TableSortOrder_.description: Declared sort order of the stored records. Each element is a column name; a leading "-" prefix denotes descending order (e.g., ["-Age", "Height"] means descending Age then ascending Height). Parsers may rely on this to skip re-sorting for binary-search or merge operations.

EnumDataDef.title: EnumData
EnumDataDef.description: Enumeration (categorical) data structure for arrays with repeated values from a finite set

_EnumKey_.description: Array of unique category values (may have mixed data types)

oneOf.description: 1-D integer array of 1-based indices into _EnumKey_
N-D annotated integer array of 1-based indices (may use array compression)

_EnumValue_.description: 1-based indices into _EnumKey_. Note: this differs from 0-based indexing used by JSONPath and Apache Arrow DictionaryArray; subtract 1 when converting.

_EnumOrdered_.description: When true, categories in _EnumKey_ are treated as an ordered (ordinal) sequence where position defines ranking (first entry is lowest). Equivalent to ordered=True in pandas CategoricalDtype or an R ordered factor. If absent or false, the enumeration is unordered (nominal).

TreeNodeContainerDef.title: TreeNodeContainer
TreeNodeContainerDef.description: Container for a tree node with optional children

LinkedListDef.title: LinkedList
LinkedListDef.description: Singly or doubly linked list structure. The container key may carry inline metadata to declare the node count: "_LinkedList_(name)::Length=N".

^_ListNode_\\(.+\\)$.description: Node data with unique name within the list

_ListNext_.description: Name of the next node (null if this is the last node)

_ListPrior_.description: Name of the previous node (null if this is the first node)

GraphDataDef.title: GraphData
GraphDataDef.description: Graph data structure with nodes and edges

_GraphNodes_.description: Graph nodes with their associated data

additionalItems.description: Optional edge data (scalar edge data is interpreted as edge weight)

_GraphEdges_.description: Directed graph edges. Each entry is [source, destination, optional_data].

additionalItems.description: Optional edge data (scalar edge data is interpreted as edge weight)

_GraphEdges0_.description: Undirected graph edges. Each entry [A, B, ...] implies the reverse edge [B, A, ...]; parsers must not double-store edge data. _GraphMatrix_ is symmetric when paired with _GraphEdges0_.

oneOf.description: Adjacency matrix in direct 2-D array format
Adjacency matrix in annotated JData array format (supports sparse representation)

_GraphMatrix_.description: Graph adjacency matrix. Interpreted as symmetric when paired with _GraphEdges0_.

oneOf.description: Base64-encoded byte stream (simple form)

MediaType.description: MIME type of the original data (e.g., "image/png")

Encoding.description: Ordered list of encoding steps applied to the byte stream

ByteLength.description: Length of the encoded data in bytes
